title: Set up task tracking backup/restore discipline
description: Configure beads to auto-export to git-tracked issues.jsonl and document the bootstrap workflow in AGENTS.md so task state survives clones and is recoverable without a Dolt remote.
acceptance_criteria:
1) .beads/config.yaml has backup.enabled: true (or equivalent) so JSONL is regularly written.
2) AGENTS.md at repo root includes a \"Task tracking\" section covering: create workflow, bootstrap-from-JSONL on clone, commit conventions.
3) Running bd bootstrap after a fresh clone successfully restores all open issues from the JSONL.
4) No Dolt remote dependency — everything works offline from the git-tracked JSONL.

title: Make watched providers/models configurable via external file
description: Add support for loading watched providers/models from an external config file instead of hardcoded values. Provide a simple selection mechanism so users can choose which provider/model IDs are monitored without editing the script.
acceptance_criteria:
1) models-watch.sh accepts a config file input (flag or documented default path).
2) Config format supports selecting specific provider/model pairs.
3) When config is present, only configured models are tracked.
4) Invalid config yields a clear error and non-zero exit.
5) Acceptance tests cover configured selection and invalid-config handling.
6) README documents usage and config examples.

title: Add provider prefixes to models-watch notifications and reports
description: Update models-watch so watched model identity is stored and compared as provider/model-id, and notifications plus --report output show provider-prefixed model ids for added, removed, and changed models. Keep existing historical delta files readable without backfilling provider data.
acceptance_criteria:
New snapshots/deltas use provider-prefixed ids for watched models.
Notify-file and popup messages show OpenCode Go/<id> or OpenCode Zen/<id>.
--report shows provider-prefixed ids for new delta files.
--report still reads legacy delta files without failing.
Tests cover add/remove/change output for both providers.

title: models-watch: detect opencode-go model changes from models.dev
description:
Create a self-contained Bash CLI that polls https://models.dev/api.json, extracts the opencode-go model block, compares model IDs against the last snapshot, writes a minimal JSON delta file only when models are added or removed, and shows a macOS popup on change.

Full plan: docs/plans/2026-04-29-models-watch.md
